refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model to reflect current equipment state, environmental conditions, and operational events with a p95 latency not exceeding the customer-defined target (baseline 1.0 s) across all supported update scenarios.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-007 requires clarification of scope (what constitutes 'worksite model'), measurement basis (p95 latency across which scenarios), and customer-specific acceptance criteria. The refined FR explicitly bounds the latency requirement to a statistical percentile and acknowledges customer tailoring, critical for high-safety-criticality systems where deterministic guarantees matter.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrument worksite model update pipeline with end-to-end latency telemetry; measure p95 latency across representative scenarios (equipment state change, sensor event, tele-operator command, safety alert); compare against customer acceptance criteria; validate under nominal and degraded network conditions.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-007.1: Capture and transmit equipment state changes (position, orientation, operational mode) with minimal latency.
FR-NGHE-007.2: Ingest and process worksite sensor data (environmental, proximity, hazard detection) within latency budget.
FR-NGHE-007.3: Propagate safety-critical updates (alerts, policy violations) with priority handling to meet latency target.
FR-NGHE-007.4: Synchronize worksite model across all stakeholder interfaces (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager) within latency bound.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized worksite model server with real-time event streaming and priority-based message queuing; all updates flow through single authoritative model instance.
DP-B: Distributed worksite model with eventual consistency; edge caching at vehicle and teleoperation nodes; periodic reconciliation via delta sync.

DP-C: Hybrid hierarchical model: authoritative core model on backend with local replicas at vehicle and teleoperation; push-based updates for safety-critical events, pull-based for non-critical state.
DP-D: Event-sourced worksite model with immutable event log; model state reconstructed on-demand at each stakeholder endpoint; latency managed via event replay optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid hierarchical model with authoritative backend, local replicas at vehicle and teleoperation nodes, push-based safety-critical updates, and pull-based non-critical state synchronization.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle telemetry capture rate and backend model update frequency (mitigated by buffering and batching).
Coupling between network link reliability and update propagation latency (mitigated by priority queuing and fallback to pull-based sync).
Coupling between safety alert generation and model consistency (mitigated by explicit safety-critical push channel).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C decouples latency-sensitive safety updates from bulk state synchronization, reducing coupling between functional requirements. Hierarchical design allows independent optimization of backend consistency and edge responsiveness. Acceptable coupling remains between network link quality and achievable p95 latency.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network link supports both push (for safety-critical) and pull (for bulk state) channels without contention. Risk: Congestion on shared network may degrade both channels; mitigation: QoS policies and bandwidth reservation.

Assumption: Vehicle and teleoperation nodes have sufficient local storage and compute for model replica. Risk: Memory/CPU constraints on edge devices; mitigation: model compression and incremental sync.

Assumption: Eventual consistency window (time to full synchronization) is acceptable for non-critical state. Risk: Stale state may confuse operators; mitigation: explicit staleness indicators and refresh-on-demand.
Assumption: Backend model server is highly available. Risk: Single point of failure; mitigation: redundancy and failover strategy (out of scope for this level).
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound but requires explicit specification of: (1) push vs. pull decision criteria (which updates are safety-critical?), (2) acceptable consistency window for non-critical state, (3) network QoS requirements to guarantee p95 latency, (4) model replica scope at each edge node (full model or subset?).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: End-to-end worksite model update pipeline: data ingestion from Vehicle and Teleoperation nodes, authoritative model state management, update propagation to all stakeholder interfaces (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager), and latency monitoring/enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Equipment state, sensor data, operational events from Vehicle and Teleoperation nodes; includes both safety-critical and non-critical updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Updated worksite model state and operational directives pushed to Vehicle and Teleoperation nodes; includes priority-based safety-critical updates and bulk state synchronization.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical updates (policy violations, hazard detections, emergency stops) propagated with priority to Safety Services and all stakeholder interfaces.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying network transport for all telemetry ingestion and model update propagation; subject to latency and reliability constraints.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration point; worksite model update is a core system function. Containment is high because the entire FR-DP mapping (model ingestion, state management, propagation) logically resides at system level. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link transport is external.

Vehicle is a data source (telemetry producer) and consumer (tasking receiver), but does not own the authoritative worksite model or update propagation logic. Assigning IM to Vehicle would scatter model management across edge nodes, violating single-source-of-truth principle and increasing diffusion.

Teleoperation is a stakeholder interface and data source, but not the owner of worksite model state or update orchestration. Assigning IM to Teleoperation would create coupling between tele-operator interface and backend model logic, increasing diffusion and complicating multi-stakeholder synchronization.

Safety Services consumes safety-critical updates (SafetyAlert interface) but does not own the worksite model or update pipeline. Safety Services is a consumer of latency-critical updates, not the producer/orchestrator. Assigning IM to Safety Services would conflate safety policy enforcement with model state management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The worksite model update pipeline is a system-level orchestration function that must integrate telemetry ingestion (Vehicle, Teleoperation), authoritative state management, and multi-stakeholder propagation (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager). NGHE Solution is the only module with sufficient scope and authority to own this end-to-end function. Vehicle and Teleoperation are data sources/consumers, not owners. Safety Services is a consumer of safety-critical updates, not the orchestrator.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module boundary may be too broad, conflating multiple concerns (model state, network transport, stakeholder interface adaptation). Mitigation: Decompose at next level into sub-modules (Model Manager, Update Dispatcher, Interface Adapter).

Risk: Network Link is external; latency guarantees depend on network QoS, which may be outside NGHE Solution control. Mitigation: Explicit network SLA requirements and fallback strategies (e.g., local caching, priority queuing).

Risk: Eventual consistency model for non-critical state may not be acceptable to all stakeholders. Mitigation: Validate consistency requirements with Site Manager and Safety Manager during requirements review.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate at this level. Recommend next-level decomposition to identify sub-modules (e.g., Worksite Model Manager, Update Dispatcher, Network Adapter) and clarify ownership of consistency, latency enforcement, and stakeholder interface adaptation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR clearly specifies latency target (p95 ≤ customer-defined, baseline 1.0 s) and scope (worksite model update across all scenarios). DP-C (hybrid hierarchical model) directly addresses latency by decoupling safety-critical push updates from bulk state sync, enabling independent optimization. DP-C is verifiable: latency can be measured end-to-end across representative scenarios. No functional gap identified.

diffusion_risks: DP-C requires coordination between backend model server and edge replicas (Vehicle, Teleoperation); this coordination logic must be clearly owned. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns orchestration; Vehicle and Teleoperation own local replica management.
Network Link is external; DP-C depends on network QoS for latency guarantees. Mitigation: Explicit network SLA requirements and fallback strategies (local caching, priority queuing).
Safety-critical push channel (SafetyAlert interface) must be isolated from bulk state sync to prevent contention. Mitigation: Separate message queues and priority handling in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: DP-C is well-contained within NGHE Solution. The hybrid hierarchical design (backend + edge replicas) maps cleanly to NGHE Solution ownership of backend model and coordination of edge synchronization. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined. No significant diffusion of DP logic across modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency verification is feasible: instrument worksite model update pipeline with end-to-end telemetry; measure p95 latency across representative scenarios (equipment state change, sensor event, tele-operator command, safety alert); compare against customer acceptance criteria. Verification can be performed in lab (simulated network) and field (real network conditions).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-007 is allocated to NGHE Solution with clear ownership of worksite model state, update orchestration, and latency enforcement. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-007.1 through 007.4) are allocated to Vehicle (telemetry capture), Teleoperation (sensor ingestion), Safety Services (safety-critical propagation), and NGHE Solution (model synchronization). Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Clarify customer-specific latency target (baseline 1.0 s p95 or customer-defined?); document acceptance criteria in requirements specification.
Define push vs. pull decision criteria: which updates are safety-critical and require push-based propagation? Which are non-critical and can use pull-based sync?
Specify acceptable consistency window for non-critical state; define staleness indicators and refresh-on-demand mechanisms.
Document network QoS requirements (bandwidth, latency, reliability) needed to support p95 latency target; identify fallback strategies if network SLA is not met.

Decompose NGHE Solution at next level to identify sub-modules (Model Manager, Update Dispatcher, Interface Adapter) and clarify ownership of consistency, latency enforcement, and stakeholder interface adaptation.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-007 is refined, DP-C is sound, and IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate. However, the FR is not yet atomic: it encompasses multiple sub-concerns (telemetry capture, model state management, safety-critical propagation, multi-stakeholder synchronization). Decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-007.1 through 007.4) is necessary to allocate responsibility and enable detailed design. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-007.1: Capture and transmit equipment state changes (position, orientation, operational mode) with minimal latency.
FR-NGHE-007.2: Ingest and process worksite sensor data (environmental, proximity, hazard detection) within latency budget.
FR-NGHE-007.3: Propagate safety-critical updates (alerts, policy violations) with priority handling to meet latency target.
FR-NGHE-007.4: Synchronize worksite model across all stakeholder interfaces (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager) within latency bound.

assumptions: Network link supports both push (safety-critical) and pull (bulk state) channels without contention.
Vehicle and Teleoperation nodes have sufficient local storage and compute for model replica.
Eventual consistency window for non-critical state is acceptable to stakeholders.
Backend model server is highly available (redundancy/failover strategy out of scope for this level).
Customer-specific latency target is defined and documented (baseline 1.0 s p95 or alternative).
Safety-critical updates are clearly distinguished from non-critical state changes.
open_risks: Network congestion may degrade both push and pull channels; QoS policies and bandwidth reservation required.
Memory/CPU constraints on edge devices (Vehicle, Teleoperation) may limit model replica scope; compression and incremental sync strategies needed.
Stale state at edge nodes may confuse operators; staleness indicators and refresh-on-demand mechanisms required.
Single point of failure at backend model server; redundancy and failover strategy required.
Consistency window for non-critical state not yet defined; stakeholder acceptance uncertain.
Network SLA requirements not yet specified; achievable p95 latency depends on network infrastructure.
evidence_needed: Customer acceptance criteria for worksite model update latency (p95 target, supported scenarios).
Network infrastructure specifications (bandwidth, latency, reliability) available at worksite.
Definition of 'safety-critical' updates vs. non-critical state changes; push vs. pull decision criteria.
Acceptable consistency window for non-critical state; staleness tolerance from Site Manager and Operator.
Vehicle and Teleoperation node specifications (storage, compute, network interface) for model replica feasibility.
Worksite model schema and update frequency estimates (equipment state, sensor data, operational events).
Latency measurement methodology and instrumentation strategy for verification.
